title: The 10 Best Herbal Remedies for Dark Circles and Puffy Eyes
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dark circles are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the appearance of dark circles by reducing puffiness, soothing irritated skin, and improving circulation. The best herbal teas for dark circles are Ginkgo biloba, Aloe barbadensis, Rosmarinus officinalis, Camellia sinensis, Hypericum perforatum, Echinacea purpurea, Silybum marianum, Vitis vinifera, Avena sativa, and Zingiber officinale. If left untreated, dark circles can lead to low self-esteem, social withdrawal, and even affect one's professional life, causing sleep deprivation, fatigue, and difficulty concentrating, ultimately impacting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular remedy for dark circles under the eyes. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which help improve blood flow to the skin. Dark circles are often caused by poor circulation and weak blood vessels, so Ginkgo biloba tea works to strengthen these vessels and increase oxygen delivery to the skin. This improves the skin's appearance, reducing the appearance of dark circles. Ginkgo biloba also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote healthy skin cell growth. When consumed as a tea, Ginkgo biloba helps to reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, giving the face a more rested and refreshed look. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to calm and soothe the skin, further reducing the appearance of dark circles.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin, also known as Ginger, is good for dark circles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes.

Kaempferol is good for dark circles because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and improve blood circulation under the eyes, thereby reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Isorhapontigenin (Ginger) is good for dark circles because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce puffiness and darkening of the skin under the eyes.

remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for dark circles
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal teas that help with dark circles, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need fresh ginger roots, boiling water, and a strainer or tea infuser. Choose a fresh and firm ginger root, wash it under cold running water, and peel its skin using a vegetable peeler. Slice the ginger into thin rounds, about 1/8 inch thick. Next, boil water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Place the sliced ginger into a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, and pour the simmering water over it. Allow the ginger to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has reduced slightly and the flavor has become strong. Strain the tea using a strainer or tea infuser, and discard the ginger slices. Your Zingiber officinale herbal tea is now ready to drink. To help with dark circles, drink 1-2 cups of this tea daily, preferably before bedtime, and combine it with a healthy diet and sufficient sleep to see noticeable results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is a natural remedy that may help to reduce dark circles under the eyes. Dark circles are caused by poor blood circulation and puffiness, which can make the skin look blue or purple. Rosemary tea has antioxidants called polyphenols, which help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. When you drink rosemary tea, the antioxidants are absorbed into the bloodstream and can help to improve circulation to the face and eyes. This can help to reduce puffiness and dark circles by bringing oxygen and nutrients to the skin. Additionally, rosemary tea may also help to improve drainage of excess fluids from the skin, which can contribute to puffiness. By improving circulation and reducing inflammation, rosemary tea may help to give the skin a more refreshed and healthy appearance.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid, also known as ginger, is good for dark circles because it has antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can help to get rid of the dark circles under the eyes.

Tannins (Ginger) are good for dark circles because they have strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect skin cells from damage, which can cause dark circles to appear under the eyes.

Polyphenols, also known as Ginger, are good for dark circles because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which in turn reduces the visibility of dark circles under the eyes.

Quercetin, also known as Ginger, is good for dark circles because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation around the eyes, which can help lighten and reduce the appearance of dark circles.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for dark circles
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal tea that helps with dark circles, you'll need some fresh ginger roots and a few simple ingredients. First, peel and slice one to two inches of fresh ginger root into thin pieces. Then, boil one cup of water in a pot and add the sliced ginger to it. Let it simmer for about 5-7 minutes, allowing the ginger to release its beneficial properties into the water. After the simmering time, turn off the heat and let the tea steep for a few more minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the ginger pieces. Finally, you can add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and a bit of honey to taste, if desired. This ginger tea is known to improve circulation, reduce puffiness, and help alleviate dark circles under the eyes. You can drink it warm or cold, and it's recommended to consume it before bedtime for optimal results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tea, also known as St. John's Wort, is a herbal remedy that may help reduce dark circles under the eyes. The tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes. This can reduce puffiness and swelling that can make dark circles appear more prominent. Additionally, the tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. Hypericum perforatum also has a natural ability to improve circulation and boost the production of collagen, a protein that helps keep skin firm and healthy. By improving skin health and reducing puffiness, Hypericum perforatum tea may help minimize the appearance of dark circles and give the skin a more even and radiant appearance.

Kaempferol is good for dark circles because it has powerful antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and increase blood flow, which in turn brighten and lighten the dark areas under the eyes.

remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal teas for dark circles
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal tea that helps with dark circles, start by gathering fresh ginger roots. Wash the roots thoroughly, then peel and slice them thinly. In a medium-sized saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2-3 slices of peeled ginger. Heat the mixture over low heat, allowing the ginger to infuse into the water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the ginger slices. Let the tea cool down, then refrigerate it for at least 30 minutes before serving. To use the tea, soak two cotton pads in the chilled tea and place them over your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in ginger can help reduce puffiness and dark circles around the eyes. You can repeat this process 2-3 times a week to achieve better results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus, also known as blueberry, is a type of tea that may help reduce dark circles under the eyes. The antioxidants in blueberry tea can help fight off free radicals that cause inflammation and damage to the delicate skin around the eyes. This can lead to puffiness and discoloration, making dark circles appear more prominent. The antioxidants in blueberry tea can also help improve blood circulation, which can help reduce the appearance of dark circles by bringing more oxygen and nutrients to the skin. Additionally, blueberry tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness under the eyes, making it a potential remedy for dark circles. Drinking blueberry tea regularly may also help improve the overall health of the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines, wrinkles, and other signs of aging.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal tea that helps with dark circles, you will need fresh ginger roots, a pot, water, and a strainer. First, peel the ginger roots and slice them into thin pieces. Then, boil about a liter of water in the pot and add the sliced ginger pieces to it. Let the mixture simmer for about 10 minutes to allow the ginger's active ingredients to infuse into the water. After the simmering time is over, turn off the heat and let the tea steep for a few minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer and discard the ginger pieces. You can add honey or lemon to the tea for taste. Once the tea has cooled down, you can drink it to help reduce dark circles under your eyes. It's recommended to drink this tea once or twice a day to see noticeable results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is a natural remedy that can help reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes. This is because the tea contains antioxidants and vitamins that can help fight inflammation and promote healthy skin. Dark circles are often caused by poor circulation, which can cause blood to pool under the eyes. Aloe barbadensis tea helps improve circulation by widening blood vessels and increasing blood flow. This can help reduce the appearance of dark circles by allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach the skin more easily. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of aloe barbadensis tea can help reduce puffiness and swelling, which can also contribute to the appearance of dark circles. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy circulation, aloe barbadensis tea can help create a more even and healthy-looking complexion. The tea can also help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation that can make dark circles more noticeable.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal tea that helps with dark circles, start by gathering 2-3 slices of fresh ginger root, about 1 inch long each. Wash them clean with water and pat dry with a towel. Then, boil a cup of water and add the ginger slices. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the water turns light brown and the aroma of ginger fills the air. Remove the tea from heat and let it cool for a minute. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the ginger slices. Add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice, if desired, and drink the tea warm. Drink this tea 2-3 times a week to help reduce dark circles under your eyes, as the anti-inflammatory properties of ginger can help improve blood circulation and reduce puffiness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tea, also known as lemon balm tea, is a soothing and calming drink that can help reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes. The tea is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. These free radicals can lead to inflammation, which contributes to the darkening of the skin under the eyes. By drinking lemon balm tea, you can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness, making the area under the eyes appear smoother and less puffy. Drinking lemon balm tea regularly can also help to improve sleep quality, which is essential for getting rid of dark circles. When you get enough sleep, your body has time to repair and rejuvenate the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles.

Start by rinsing the ginger root under cold running water, then chop it into small pieces to release its flavor and nutrients. Next, boil a cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop, and then reduce the heat to a simmer. After the steeping time has passed, carefully remove the ginger root from the water, and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea into a cup and serve, or store it in the refrigerator for later use. To get the most out of this tea for dark circles, drink it once or twice a day, either hot or cold, and combine it with a healthy lifestyle that includes a balanced diet, regular exercise, and sufficient sleep to help reduce the appearance of dark circles.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal tea that helps with dark circles, start by gathering 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginger root or 1-inch piece of fresh ginger. Next, boil 8 ounces of water in a pot and let it cool for a minute. Then, add the dried or fresh ginger to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and nutrients to infuse. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the ginger particles. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's best to consume the tea without any additives for its full benefits. The Zingiber officinale tea is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes by improving blood circulation and reducing water retention.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis tea, also known as green tea, is a great help for dark circles. This is because green tea has antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and swelling under the eyes. When we get less sleep or don't eat well, our blood vessels can get bigger and more visible, making dark circles look worse. Green tea's antioxidants can help shrink these blood vessels, reducing their visibility. Additionally, the caffeine in green tea can help constrict blood vessels and improve circulation, which can make the skin look smoother and more even. This can make the dark circles less noticeable. Green tea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness and swelling, making the area look more refreshed and awake.

Strain the tea into a cup and discard the ginger root. To enhance its effectiveness in reducing dark circles, you can add a few slices of cucumber or a few ice cubes to the tea before drinking it. Finally, consume the tea warm, 2-3 times a day, to help improve blood circulation and reduce the appearance of dark circles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a herbal remedy that may help with dark circles under the eyes. The antioxidants in this tea help to fight free radicals that cause oxidative stress, which can lead to dark circles. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness and swelling, making it easier to see the underlying skin. Echinacea purpurea tea is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids that improve blood circulation and reduce water retention, both of which contribute to dark circles. By improving blood flow and reducing puffiness, Echinacea purpurea tea can help to even out skin tone and make dark circles appear less noticeable.

Drinking this tea regularly may help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes, thanks to ginger's natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve blood circulation.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal tea that helps with dark circles, start by gathering fresh ginger roots. Wash them clean with cold water, then peel and slice them into thin pieces. Place the sliced ginger in a saucepan with two cups of water and bring to a boil over medium heat. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the ginger to release its active compounds. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for 5 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the ginger pieces. You can add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and a bit of honey to taste. Drink the tea 2-3 times a week, ideally before bedtime, to help reduce the appearance of dark circles under your eyes. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in ginger are believed to improve circulation and reduce puffiness, giving you a more refreshed and revitalized look.

supplementary_best: Combining Rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary), Melissa officinalis (lemon balm), and Avena sativa (oat straw) herbal teas may help remedy dark circles.  Rosmarinus officinalis has antioxidant properties that can reduce oxidative stress, which contributes to dark circles. It improves circulation and reduces puffiness by constricting blood vessels.  Melissa officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing swelling and redness. Its calming effects can also help regulate sleep patterns, which is essential in reducing dark circles.  Avena sativa, or oat straw, is a natural anti-inflammatory agent that can reduce puffiness and swelling. It also has a soothing effect on the skin, which can help improve circulation and reduce the appearance of dark circles.  This combination of herbal teas is effective because it addresses multiple aspects of dark circles, including puffiness, swelling, and oxidative stress.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dark circles, it's best to avoid herbal teas like Ashwagandha. This is because Ashwagandha can cause blood vessels to dilate, which may make your dark circles look worse. Another tea to avoid is Dandelion root tea. Although it can help reduce water retention, which can contribute to puffy eyes, it can also cause your body to lose potassium. This can be a problem if you already have dark circles, as a potassium deficiency can make them look even darker.  Licorice root tea is also something to be cautious about. It can raise blood pressure, which may make your dark circles more noticeable. Additionally, licorice root can cause an increase in estrogen levels, which may worsen dark circles in some people.  Lastly, avoid Sage tea. Sage can cause your blood vessels to constrict, which may reduce blood flow to the eyes. This can make dark circles look more prominent.
supplementary_other_remedies: Cucumber slices can help reduce dark circles under the eyes. The cooling effect of the cucumber helps to constrict blood vessels, which reduces puffiness and makes the skin appear lighter. This is because when blood vessels are constricted, less blood flows to the area, which in turn reduces the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, cucumber has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and reduce swelling.  Cold compresses can also be used to help alleviate dark circles. When applied to the skin, cold temperatures cause blood vessels to constrict, which has the same effect as cucumber slices. This can be done by wetting a cloth with cold water, wringing it out, and applying it to the affected area for a few minutes. The cold temperature helps to reduce swelling and puffiness, making the dark circles appear less noticeable.  Eye masks can also help with dark circles. These masks are typically made of gel or cream and are designed to be placed under the eyes. They often contain ingredients such as caffeine or peptides that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness. When applied to the skin, these masks can help to reduce the appearance of dark circles by reducing the amount of blood flowing to the area.  Potato slices can also be used to help reduce dark circles. Similar to cucumber slices, potato slices have a cooling effect that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness. The starch in potatoes also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and reduce swelling. To use potato slices, simply slice a raw potato and place the slices under your eyes for a few minutes.  Eye creams that contain vitamin K can also help with dark circles. Vitamin K is an antioxidant that can help improve circulation and reduce the appearance of dark circles. When applied to the skin, vitamin K can help to reduce the amount of blood flowing to the area, which in turn reduces the appearance of dark circles. Eye creams that contain vitamin K are often used in combination with other ingredients, such as peptides or caffeine, to enhance their effectiveness.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have dark circles under their eyes often experience other health issues related to poor circulation and fatigue. They may also suffer from headaches, dry skin, and puffy eyes due to water retention. One common condition is sinusitis, an inflammation of the sinuses that can cause nasal congestion and sinus pressure. Medicinal herbs like Echinacea and Goldenseal can help alleviate sinusitis by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Another condition is insomnia or sleep disorders, which can be treated with herbs like Valerian root and Chamomile, known for their calming effects. Some people may also experience allergies, which can be alleviated with herbs like Nettle and Butterbur that have anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, dark circle sufferers may experience digestive issues like bloating and constipation due to poor circulation and stress. Herbs like Ginger and Peppermint can help ease digestion and reduce symptoms. Additionally, dark circles may be a sign of anemia or iron deficiency, which can be treated with herbs like Dandelion and Alfalfa rich in iron and other essential nutrients.
